mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Cluster API chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm, which includes the necessary Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command applies the Cluster API components.

steps.title: Create a management cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a management cluster that will be used to manage other clusters.

```bash

clusterctl init --infrastructure aws

```

> Replace 'aws' with your desired infrastructure provider.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> This command lists all pods in the kube-system namespace to verify that the Cluster API components are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and warns about potential data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

helm uninstall cluster-api

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your management cluster. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation afterward.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -A > backup-clusters.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup the current cluster state.
> Apply the new version of Cluster API components.
> Verify that the new components are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues or the server being down.

**Fix:**
Check the network configuration and ensure the API server is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Cluster CRD is not installed or not available in the current context.

**Fix:**
Reapply the Cluster API components using the provided YAML file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to create control plane'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The control plane components may not be starting due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the cluster has sufficient resources allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to reconcile Cluster: no provider found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the correct infrastructure provider is installed and configured.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for AWS and Azure infrastructure providers, added new features for cluster scaling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older Kubernetes versions below 1.26.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your infrastructure provider configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your managed clusters and their statuses.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
